message:
perf(lexical-rebuild): batch message fetching, multi-threshold commit triggers, and historical salvage refinement

Three interrelated performance and correctness improvements to the
lexical rebuild pipeline:

## Batch message fetching (storage/sqlite.rs, indexer/mod.rs)

Add fetch_messages_for_lexical_rebuild_batch() which retrieves messages
for an entire batch of conversations in a single SQL query using
IN (?) with conversation_ids, returning a HashMap<i64, Vec<Message>>
grouped by conversation_id. The lexical rebuild loop now calls this
once per batch instead of calling fetch_messages_for_lexical_rebuild()
once per conversation, eliminating N round-trips per batch where N is
the page size (typically 1000). Messages are consumed from the HashMap
via remove() so they are freed as each conversation is processed.

Includes a test verifying grouping correctness and that extra_json is
null in the lightweight projection.

## Multi-threshold Tantivy commit triggers (indexer/mod.rs)

The rebuild commit interval logic previously triggered only on
conversations-processed count. This is insufficient for databases
with highly skewed message distributions — a single conversation with
100K messages could accumulate unbounded Tantivy writer memory before
hitting the conversation threshold.

New commit triggers (any one sufficient):
- CASS_TANTIVY_REBUILD_COMMIT_EVERY_CONVERSATIONS (default: 1000)
- CASS_TANTIVY_REBUILD_COMMIT_EVERY_MESSAGES (default: 5000)
- CASS_TANTIVY_REBUILD_COMMIT_EVERY_MESSAGE_BYTES (default: 16 MiB)

The should_commit_lexical_rebuild() helper evaluates all three and is
checked per-conversation (not per-batch) so oversized conversations
trigger a commit immediately. The commit logic is extracted into a
reusable commit_rebuild_progress() closure.

## Historical salvage and daily_stats repair refinement (indexer/mod.rs)

- should_salvage_historical_databases() extracted as a testable function:
  salvage runs when storage was rebuilt OR canonical DB is empty, but
  NOT when opts.full is the only trigger and canonical DB is populated.
  Previously, --full always triggered salvage even when the canonical
  database was already correct, causing unnecessary re-imports.

- canonical_only_full_rebuild now also requires zero historical imports
  (historical_salvage.conversations_imported == 0) before skipping the
  full scan, preventing data loss when historical bundles contribute
  new conversations during a --full rebuild.

- daily_stats repair is deferred during canonical-only full rebuilds
  (the rebuild itself will produce correct stats), but runs when
  historical salvage imports conversations.

## Daily stats message scanning (storage/sqlite.rs)

Simplify the daily stats message scan to iterate per-conversation
rather than per-batch-range. The query now filters by a single
conversation_id with a cursor on message idx, removing the
conversation_id BETWEEN range filter and the HashMap lookup for
per-conversation metadata. This eliminates the temp B-tree SQLite
would create for the range-keyset pagination and removes the
"conversation outside active batch" error path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
